place: East Hampton North CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: East Hampton North was first settled by farmers and fishermen in 1648, who crossed Long Island Sound to establish the community.
The area originally called Good Ground was settled in 1740 and later became the main hamlet among eleven in the region.
In 1922, the eleven hamlets—including Good Ground—merged under the name Hampton Bays to tap into the growing Hamptons trade.
The Shinnecock Inlet, which opens to the Atlantic, was formed during the 1938 New England hurricane when storm forces reshaped the barrier beach.
Commercial fishing remains a vital part of the local economy, centered around the fishing station at Shinnecock Inlet.
East Hampton North CDP is bounded by the Atlantic Ocean to the south, Peconic Bay to the north, and Shinnecock Bay to the east.
